description: Airflow worker pod resource utilisation — what are we wasting, and what should our limits be.

panels.title: OOM Kills
panels.description: Pods killed due to memory exhaustion in the selected time range. These tasks have limits set too low for their peak usage.

panels.title: Under-Provisioned Tasks
panels.description: Tasks where peak memory > 80% of their limit. These are at OOM risk — their limit or pod size needs to increase.

panels.title: Over-Provisioned Tasks
panels.description: Tasks using less than 20% of their memory limit on average. These are wasting cluster resources.

panels.title: Active Worker Pods
panels.description: Worker pods currently running with a base container and valid dag_id + task_id labels.

panels.title: Active DAG / Task Combinations
panels.description: Distinct dag_id + task_id pairs currently running.

panels.title: Nodes >85% Memory Used
panels.description: Nodes running airflow workers where memory usage exceeds 85% of total node memory. High node memory = worker pods at risk of eviction even if their own limits aren't hit.

panels.title: Nodes >80% CPU Used
panels.description: Nodes running airflow workers where CPU usage exceeds 80% of total. High node CPU = worker tasks being throttled even if their pod CPU limit isn't reached.

panels.title: Fleet Memory by Pod Size — Stacked
panels.description: Total memory consumed per pod size over time. The height of each band shows how much that size contributes. A dominant band means that tier drives your memory bill.

panels.title: Fleet CPU by Pod Size — Stacked
panels.description: Total CPU consumed per pod size over time. Which tier is driving your CPU usage?

targets.expr:
avg by (label_pod_size) (
  rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{namespace="$namespace", container="base"}[$__rate_interval])
  * on(pod) group_left(label_pod_size)
  label_replace(
  kube_pod_labels{namespace="$namespace", label_dag_id=~"$dag_id", label_task_id=~"$task_id", label_pod_size=~"$pod_size", label_dag_id!="", label_task_id!=""},
  "label_pod_size", "unknown", "label_pod_size", "^$"
)
)

panels.title: 🔬 Right-Sizing Detail — Set Your Limits Here
Memory Right-Sizing — All Tasks
panels.description: Every active task sorted by Peak % of Limit. Use Avg Memory to set your Request. Use Peak Memory to set your Limit. Peak % yellow >80% = OOM risk, red >100% = will OOM. Avg % blue <20% = over-provisioned (wasting resources).

panels.title: CPU Right-Sizing — All Tasks
panels.description: Every active task sorted by Peak % of Limit. Use Avg CPU to set your Request. Use Peak CPU to set your Limit. Unlike memory, hitting the CPU limit throttles the task — it slows down but is not killed.

targets.expr:
label_replace(
  label_replace(
    label_replace(
      avg by (label_dag_id, label_task_id, label_pod_size) (
  rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{namespace="$namespace", container="base"}[$__rate_interval])
  * on(pod) group_left(label_dag_id, label_task_id, label_pod_size)
  label_replace(
  kube_pod_labels{namespace="$namespace", label_dag_id=~"$dag_id", label_task_id=~"$task_id", label_pod_size=~"$pod_size", label_dag_id!="", label_task_id!=""},
  "label_pod_size", "unknown", "label_pod_size", "^$"
)
),
      "task_key", "$1", "label_dag_id", "(.*)"
    ),
    "task_key", "${task_key} | $1", "label_task_id", "(.*)"
  ),
  "task_key", "${task_key} | $1", "label_pod_size", "(.*)"
)

label_replace(
  label_replace(
    label_replace(
      max by (label_dag_id, label_task_id, label_pod_size) (
  rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{namespace="$namespace", container="base"}[$__rate_interval])
  * on(pod) group_left(label_dag_id, label_task_id, label_pod_size)
  label_replace(
  kube_pod_labels{namespace="$namespace", label_dag_id=~"$dag_id", label_task_id=~"$task_id", label_pod_size=~"$pod_size", label_dag_id!="", label_task_id!=""},
  "label_pod_size", "unknown", "label_pod_size", "^$"
)
),
      "task_key", "$1", "label_dag_id", "(.*)"
    ),
    "task_key", "${task_key} | $1", "label_task_id", "(.*)"
  ),
  "task_key", "${task_key} | $1", "label_pod_size", "(.*)"
)

panels.title: 📈 Usage Trends — Is It Getting Worse?
Avg Memory Usage — by DAG / Task
panels.description: Memory trend per task over the selected time window. Use the DAG ID and Task ID filters to focus. A growing line across runs = memory leak or increasing data volume.

panels.title: Avg CPU Usage — by DAG / Task
panels.description: CPU trend per task over the selected time window. A task consistently near its CPU limit is being throttled and will run slower than expected.

targets.expr:
avg by (label_dag_id, label_task_id) (
  rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{namespace="$namespace", container="base"}[$__rate_interval])
  * on(pod) group_left(label_dag_id, label_task_id)
  label_replace(
  kube_pod_labels{namespace="$namespace", label_dag_id=~"$dag_id", label_task_id=~"$task_id", label_pod_size=~"$pod_size", label_dag_id!="", label_task_id!=""},
  "label_pod_size", "unknown", "label_pod_size", "^$"
)
)

panels.title: OOM Kills by Task × Pod Size
panels.description: OOM kills per task per pod size. A task OOM-ing on small but not medium = resize it. OOM-ing on all sizes = limit is fundamentally too low.

panels.title: OOM Kill Rate — Over Time
panels.description: OOM events over time. Correlate spikes with the memory trend panel — if memory was climbing when this spiked, the limit is too low.

panels.title: 🖥️ Node Health — Where Are Workers Running?
Node Memory Used % — Worker Nodes Only
panels.description: Memory utilisation per node (only nodes running airflow workers). Yellow >85%, red >95%. If a node is near capacity, new worker pods may fail to schedule or existing pods may be evicted.

panels.title: Node CPU Used % — Worker Nodes Only
panels.description: CPU utilisation per node (only nodes running airflow workers). Yellow >80%, red >90%. High node CPU means worker tasks compete for CPU even if their pod limits appear fine.

panels.title: Node Summary — Worker Nodes
panels.description: One row per node running airflow workers. Mem Used %: yellow >85% = approaching capacity, red >95% = critical. CPU Used %: yellow >80%, red >90%. Worker Pods: how many airflow worker pods are currently on this node. Use this to spot hot nodes — if one node has many pods AND high utilisation, your scheduler is not spreading workers evenly.

panels.title: Worker Pods per Node — Over Time
panels.description: How many airflow worker pods are running on each node over time. Uneven distribution means one node bears most of the load. Spikes show when batch jobs run.

title: Airflow Worker Pods — Resource Utilisation
